description: Align all treb fork subcommand outputs with Go CLI formatting conventions: styled tables with color, stage indicators, enhanced status columns (uptime, snapshot count, deployment count), deterministic JSON output via print_json(), and updated golden files.

userStories.title: Styled Status Table with Enhanced Columns
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fork status to display styled table output with uptime, snapshot count, and deployment count columns so that I get comprehensive fork session information at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Status table includes columns: Network, Chain ID, RPC URL, Port, Fork Block, Started At, Uptime, Snapshots, Deployments, Status
Network column uses color::NAMESPACE style when color is enabled
Status column uses color::SUCCESS for running and color::ERROR for stopped
Uptime is formatted as human-readable duration (e.g., 2h 15m, 3d 1h)
Snapshots column shows entry.snapshots.len()
Deployments column shows count of fork/ namespace deployments from current registry
Unit tests for uptime formatting function
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Styled History Table and Diff Table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fork history and fork diff tables to use color styling per action/change type so that the output is visually consistent with other styled commands.
acceptanceCriteria: History table: Action column is styled per action type (enter=SUCCESS, exit=WARNING, revert=MUTED, restart=STAGE) when color is enabled
History table: Network column uses color::CHAIN style
Diff table: Change column is styled per change type (added=green, removed=red, modified=yellow)
Diff table: Key column uses color::LABEL style
styled() helper function defined locally in fork.rs following per-module convention
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Stage Indicators for Enter, Exit, Revert, and Restart
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fork enter/exit/revert/restart to display stage indicators via print_stage() so that operation progress is visually consistent with run/compose commands.
acceptanceCriteria: fork enter: stage indicators for snapshot creation and completion
fork exit: stage indicators for registry restore and cleanup
fork revert: stage indicators for EVM revert, new snapshot, and registry restore per network
fork restart: stage indicators for Anvil reset, CreateX deploy, snapshot, and registry restore
Warning banner for no EVM snapshots stored case in revert uses print_warning_banner()
All stage output goes to stderr via print_stage(), final confirmation remains on stdout
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Deterministic JSON Output via print_json
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all fork --json output to use print_json() for deterministic key ordering so that JSON output is consistent and diff-friendly.
acceptanceCriteria: fork status --json uses print_json() instead of serde_json::to_string_pretty()
fork history --json uses print_json() instead of serde_json::to_string_pretty()
fork diff --json uses print_json() instead of serde_json::to_string_pretty()
JSON output keys are alphabetically sorted at all levels
fork status --json includes new fields: uptime (string), snapshotCount (integer), deploymentCount (integer)
Typecheck passes (cargo check -p treb-cli)

userStories.title: Golden File Updates for All Fork Subcommands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all fork golden files regenerated and passing so that the test suite validates the new styled output, enhanced columns, stage indicators, and sorted JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing fork golden files regenerated with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p treb-cli
Golden files for status reflect new columns (Uptime, Snapshots, Deployments)
Golden files for enter/exit reflect stage indicator output on stderr
Golden files for JSON output reflect sorted key ordering
All cargo test -p treb-cli -- fork tests pass
No regressions in non-fork golden file tests (full cargo test -p treb-cli passes)
